result:
The rain never stops in this city, but that’s exactly why I love it. There’s something honest about water that refuses to pretend the world is dry. It slicks the neon signs until they bleed magenta and electric blue across every puddle, turning sidewalks into liquid galaxies. People hurry under black umbrellas like elegant beetles, and for a moment none of us are strangers; we’re all just trying not to drown in the same downpour.

I came here to disappear and somehow found myself instead. The apartment is tiny, the radiator clanks like an old man clearing his throat, and my neighbor practices trumpet at 2 a.m. Some nights I lie awake listening to him butcher Coltrane and feel weirdly grateful. Imperfection has become my favorite melody.

There’s a stray cat that visits the fire escape. One ear missing, tail kinked like a question mark. I leave sardines out even though I’m allergic. We have an understanding: I won’t name him and he won’t love me too much. It’s the healthiest relationship I’ve had in years.

Tomorrow I’ll probably waste another morning watching pigeons fight over a crust of bread, drinking coffee that tastes like regret and nutmeg. And it will be perfect. Not because anything extraordinary happens, but because I finally stopped waiting for extraordinary to arrive. The ordinary, it turns out, is plenty when you pay attention.

The rain just picked up. Good. Let it keep falling. I’ve got nowhere else I’d rather be.

(Word count: 248)

(Word count: 248)

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
